title: flutter_bloc

sections.markdown: `flutter_bloc` is the Flutter binding for the BLoC library — Blocs/Cubits expose immutable states via streams; widgets subscribe to and rebuild from them. Adds BlocProvider, BlocBuilder, BlocListener, BlocConsumer, RepositoryProvider, and BlocObserver.
Use BlocBuilder for UI, BlocListener for side effects (nav, snackbars), BlocConsumer for both. Add a global BlocObserver in main() to log every transition — a huge debugging + auditing win.
Sharing a Bloc across the whole app when it should be scoped to a feature. Use MultiBlocProvider at the feature route; the Bloc is disposed when the route pops.
`buildWhen`/`listenWhen` avoid noisy rebuilds/handlers. Use bloc_test to assert emitted state sequences for a given event input — one of the cleanest test stories in Flutter.

flashcards.back: Builder rebuilds UI; Listener runs side effects; Consumer does both.

flashcards.front: How to log every state transition?
flashcards.back: Register a BlocObserver in main() via Bloc.observer = MyObserver().
buildWhen / listenWhen to filter which state changes matter.
